recommendations: redundant_read_same_unit is the only bias showing confirmed worsening this cycle (5 units vs prior 3-unit plateau, all in same session 6dbfac03) — the pattern of reading the same file 2-4 times consecutively with no offset variation or intervening edit suggests file content is not being retained across back-to-back tool calls within a unit; this is distinct from the 'read before edit' discipline (which is correct behavior) and is specifically the redundant second-or-third read of a file just read; no new mechanical fix recommended beyond prior flagging, but escalation from STABLE to WORSENING warrants tracking

mcp_retry_without_diagnosis has appeared structurally identical across 6+ consecutive cycles with no change; the render MCP permission-scope issue (select_workspace succeeds but all service-level queries fail) has never been resolved at the infrastructure level and agent-side behavior has not adapted; recommending user-level render MCP auth configuration audit rather than continued agent-side flagging — this pattern cannot be resolved by agent calibration alone

shell_tool_preference_violations has exceeded 50% of tool-using units for 24+ consecutive cycles with no downward trajectory; further calibration recommendations are unlikely to produce change; the perl inline bulk-substitution pattern observed in 6dbfac03-49 (using perl -0pi via Bash for multi-line text replacement instead of Edit tool) is a new variant not previously flagged and should be noted as an extension of this bias family
